reasoning: A recent United Nations World Meteorological Organization report published on November 6, 2025—just 3 days before the knowledge cutoff—stated that 2025 is on track to be the second or third hottest year on record, falling short of 2024's record-breaking temperatures. This trend is consistently reported across multiple major outlets including Carbon Brief, Yale Climate Connections, and The Guardian. Given this near-certainty from authoritative agencies near the end of the year, the likelihood of 2025 setting a new GISTEMP annual record is essentially negligible, aligning with the market's 2% estimate.
